source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Synthetic Base Mud Spill
actions_taken: Transocean personnel stopped SBM discharge by manually pressurizing TSJ lower packer at 20:35 hours
Chevron notified National Response Center (Incident Report #1093959)
TSJ upper packer leak was fixed by taking load off lines and tightening JIC connections
event.summary: At 20:20 hours on 30 August 2014, the Transocean Discoverer Clear Leader drill ship under contract to Chevron was conducting drilling operations at Keathley Canyon Block 829 when a loss of synthetic base mud was observed from a telescopic slip joint packer failure. Approximately 12 barrels of 14.5 ppg SBM (6.12 gallons synthetic oil) was spilled into the Gulf of Mexico but was restricted to the moon pool area. Root cause was a leaking stainless steel JIC connection for the air hose at the bottom of the J-plate, with contributing factors including improper hose support, ocean currents causing vibration, and submersion of hose bundle below water line.

hazards.name: Subsea equipment exposed to ocean currents and wave action

threats.name: JIC connection failure due to inadequate hose support
Hose vibration from ocean currents causing connection loosening
Wave action transmitted through submerged hose bundle
Secondary packer system leak preventing automatic engagement

consequences.name: Synthetic base mud spill to Gulf of Mexico
Loss of pressure in TSJ upper packer

controls.name: Air hose support system for JIC connections
controls.barrier_role: Prevent mechanical stress on JIC connections
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent connection failure

supporting_text: the air hoses to the JIC connections on the J-Plate were not properly supported; therefore the weight of the hoses put stress on the JIC connections

controls.name: TSJ upper packer pressure containment
controls.barrier_role: Maintain pressure integrity to prevent SBM discharge
controls.lod_basis: Primary barrier to prevent loss of containment

supporting_text: a leaking stainless steel Joint Industry Council (JIC) connection for the air hose located at the bottom of the J-plate caused the TSJ upper packer to loose pressure thus allowing the SBM to be discharged

controls.name: TSJ lower packer automatic engagement system
controls.barrier_role: Automatically engage lower packer to contain SBM if upper packer fails
controls.lod_basis: Secondary containment barrier to mitigate consequence of upper packer failure

supporting_text: a similar leak was found on secondary packer system that inhibited the automatic engagement of the TSJ lower packer
Transocean personnel stopped the SBM discharge by manually pressurizing the TSJ lower packer

controls.name: Manual intervention - pressurization of TSJ lower packer
controls.barrier_role: Manual control to stop SBM discharge when automatic system fails
controls.lod_basis: Manual intervention by trained personnel to mitigate spill

supporting_text: At 20:35 hours, Transocean personnel stopped the SBM discharge by manually pressurizing the TSJ lower packer

controls.name: Hose bundle positioning and protection from wave action
controls.barrier_role: Prevent wave action transmission through submerged hose bundle
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent vibration-induced connection loosening

supporting_text: the hose bundle was submerged below the water line and wave action had transmitted through the hoses to hanging manifold

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
